sections.title: Welcome to Encapsulate
sections.content:
This app is your personal coach for ICT162 Object Oriented Programming.

You will learn Python OOP step by step, starting from the very basics and building up to exam-level questions.

Each lesson is short. You can stop anytime and pick up where you left off.

sections.title: What is OOP?
sections.content:
OOP stands for Object Oriented Programming. It is a way of writing code where you group related data and actions together into things called **objects**.

Think of it like this: a real-world "cash card" has a card number and a balance. In OOP, you would create a CashCard object that stores those same things.

sections.title: What you will cover
sections.content:
ICT162 covers six main topics:

1. **Classes & Objects** — the core building blocks
2. **Composition & Collection** — objects inside objects, and lists of objects
3. **Inheritance** — child classes that extend parent classes
4. **Exception Handling** — dealing with errors gracefully
5. **Python GUI** — building simple graphical interfaces with tkinter
6. **SOLID Principles** — writing clean, maintainable designs

sections.title: How lessons work
sections.content:
Each lesson has:

- **Explanations** in plain language
- **Code examples** you can study
- **Quick checks** to test your understanding
- **Practice prompts** where you try writing code

You earn XP for completing sections and answering questions. Your progress is saved automatically.

question.explanation: OOP stands for Object Oriented Programming. It is a programming style where you organise code around objects that combine data and behaviour.

sections.title: You are ready
sections.content:
That is it for the introduction. Next up: **Classes & Objects**, where you will learn how to define your first Python class.

Tip: You can always come back to any lesson from the dashboard or the Study Units page.
